english: people! Mil.; mgo dgú-ba (mgo dgu ba), dgúr-ba (dgur ba) v. dgú-ba (dgu ba), mgo ₒtóm-pa (mgo 'thom pa) v. ₒtóm-pa ('thom pa); mgor jóg-pa (mgor jog pa) to carry on the head Sch.; *go yúg-če* (go yug ce) W. to shake one's head, *kug táṅ-če* (kug tang ce) W. to nod with the head, either as a sign of affirmation, or of beckoning to a person; *kyog-kyóg čò-če* (kyog kyog co ce) to wave the head from one side to the other, expressive of reflection. — 2. summit, height, top, ri-mgo (ri mgo) Ká-bas (kha bas) gyogs (g.yogs) Mil. the hill-tops were covered with snow. — 3. first place, principal part, mgo byéd-pa (mgo byed pa) to lead, to command, to be at the head Glr.; to educate cf. dbu mdzád-pa (dbu mdzad pa); to inspect, look after, superintend, control, bú-mo žig-gis mgo byéd-pai mi máṅ-po (bu mo zhig gis mgo byed pa'i mi mang po) a number of (labouring) people looked after by a girl (the farmer's daughter) Mil.; *dos gó čò-če* (dos go co ce) W. to preside in a consultation. — 4. beginning, W., *go-ma* (go ma); grós-mgo (gros mgo) the beginning of a consultation; mgo ₒdzúg-pa (mgo 'dzug pa) to begin; bod sdúg-pai mgo ₒdzugs (bod sdug pa'i mgo 'dzugs) that was the beginning of the misfortunes of Tibet Ma; brtán-gyi skyid-mgo dé-nas tsugs (brtan gyi skyid mgo de nas tsugs) with this my constant good-fortune commenced Mil.; ló-mgo-la (lo mgo la) at the beginning of the year Mil.; mgó-nas (mgo nas) from the beginning Dzl. — 5. Gram.: a superscribed r, l, s e.g. rá-mgoi ka (ra mgo'i ka), རྐ, k with r superscribed; dé-rnams bas j́ul sá-mgoi kao (de rnams bas 'phul sa mgo'i ka'o) these are the words beginning with bsk (bsk). Comp. and deriv. mgo-klád (mgo klad) brain Cs. — mgo-dkyíl (mgo dkyil) col. crown of the head, vertex. — *go-kár* (go kar), or *gar* (gar) Ld. a tight under-garment, drawn over the head when put on, (Ssk. परिधान, Hd. प्रहिरवण) something like a shirt, but not in general use. — mgo-skór (mgo skor) imposture, deceit, bdud-kyi mgo-skór de ṅa mi ₒdod (bdud kyi mgo skor de nga mi 'dod) I detest these diabolical tricks Mil. — mgo-skyá (mgo skya) a gray head, mgo-skyá-čan (mgo skya can) a gray-headed person Cs. — *go-kyóṅ* (go kyong) C., W., protector, patron, = mgo-ₒdren (mgo 'dren). — mgo-khrá (mgo khra) scald; scald-head Sch. — mgo-mkhrégs-čan (mgo mkhregs can) obstinate, pertinacious, stubborn, esp. in buying and bartering; selfish, bargaining, haggling: *go ṭag čò-če* (go thag co ce) W. to have these qualities. — mgo-rgyán (mgo rgyan) head-ornament. — mgo-čan (mgo can) having a head, *mí-go-čan* (mi go can) having a man's head, such as English rupees and other coins (bearing the image of a head) W. — *go-čiṅ* (go cing) C., W. = go-ₒdrén (go 'dren). — *go-(l)cág* (go (l)cag) a blow or knock on the head Ld. — mgo-lčógs (mgo lcogs) little shoots, sprouts, branches Sch. — mgo-čá (mgo ca) = mgo-rgyán (mgo rgyan). — mgo-mǰug (mgo mjug) beginning and end (head and tail), šiṅ ₒdii mgo mǰug gaṅ yin-pa bye-brág pyes šig (shing 'di'i mgo mjug gang yin pa bye brag phyes shig) find out which is the upper and which the lower end of this piece of wood Dzl. — mgo-jón (mgo jon) Cs.: 'an oblong head.' — mgo-rjéṅ (mgo rjeng) bare headed. — mgo-nyág (mgo nyag) Cs.: 'a compressed, contracted head'. *go-nyí-pa* (go nyi pa) C. two-headed, double-tongued; a double-dealer, backbiter. — mgo-snyóms (mgo snyoms) indifferent, unconcerned. — *gor-tiṅ tsáṅ-ma* (gor ting tsang ma) from head to heel, the whole from top to toe, = *go-lus-ča-tsaṅ* (go lus ca tsang). — mgo-ₒdón (mgo 'don) = mgo-ₒdrén (mgo 'dren), with byed-pa (byed pa) = mgo ₒdon-pa (mgo 'don pa) to bring or draw forth, to raise, to lift up a person's head, gen. with raṅ (rang), one's own head, used in the sense of: to be self-dependent, one's own master, to come off well, to be uppermost Mil.; mgo ₒton-pa (mgo 'thon pa) id. — mgo-ₒdrén (mgo 'dren) protector, patron, used frq. in letters as a complimentary title. — mgo-nág po (mgo nag po) for man Glr. — mgo-nád (mgo nad) headache. — *gó-bu* (go bu) W. first-born. — mgó-ma (mgo ma) 1. adj. first, gral-mgo-ma (gral mgo ma) first in order, the first in a row or line of persons Mil. 2. sbst. the beginning W., *go-ma tsug-če* (go ma tsug ce) to begin. 3. adv. in the beginning, at first W. — mgo-tsém (mgo tsem) 'stitched at the head' denoting a book which is so stitched, that the lines run parallel to the back, whilst one stitched in our way is called rta-mgó-ma (rta mgo ma). — mgo-gžuṅ (mgo gzhung), col. *gog-žuṅ* (gog zhung) crown of the head. — mgou, mgeu (mgo'u, mge'u) a small head Cs. — mgo-yór (mgo yor) = tsá-bai nad (tsa ba'i nad) Ts. — mgo-gyógs (mgo g.yogs) a covering for the head (hat, cap etc.). — mgo-ríl (mgo ril) 1. a round head. 2. cattle without horns W. — mgo-rég (mgo reg) for mgo brégs-pa (mgo bregs pa) one that has his head shaved, a monk; mgo-rég btsún-ma (mgo reg btsun ma) Lt. monks and nuns, or: nuns that have their heads shaved — *go-lus-ča-tsáṅ* (go lus ca tsang) a complete suit of clothes, *gor-tiṅ-tsáṅ-ma* (gor ting tsang ma) id.; *go lus sum kón-če* (go lus sum kon ce) W. to furnish a person with new clothes; *go lus spó-če* (go lus spo ce) W. to
